1|name: Render Auto-Deploy on Push
     2|
     3|on:
     4|  push:
     5|    branches: [main]
     6|  workflow_dispatch:
     7|    inputs:
     8|      clear_cache:
     9|        description: 'Clear build cache'
    10|        required: false
    11|        default: false
    12|        type: boolean
    13|
    14|jobs:
    15|  deploy:
    16|    name: Deploy to Render
    17|    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    18|    timeout-minutes: 20
    19|    steps:
    20|      - name: Validate secrets
    21|        id: secrets
    22|        run: |
    23|          MISSING=""
    24|          if [ -z "${{ secrets.RENDER_API_KEY }}" ]; then
    25|            echo "MISSING_SECRET: RENDER_API_KEY"
    26|            MISSING="${MISSING} RENDER_API_KEY"
    27|          fi
    28|          if [ -z "${{ secrets.RENDER_SERVICE_ID }}" ]; then
    29|            echo "MISSING_SECRET: RENDER_SERVICE_ID"
    30|            MISSING="${MISSING} RENDER_SERVICE_ID"
    31|          fi
    32|          if [ -n "${MISSING}" ]; then
    33|            echo "status=blocked" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
    34|            echo "missing=${MISSING}" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
    35|            echo "::error::Missing secrets:${MISSING}"
    36|          else
    37|            echo "status=ready" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
    38|          fi
    39|
    40|      - name: Trigger Render Deploy
    41|        if: steps.secrets.outputs.status == 'ready'
    42|        id: deploy
    43|        run: |
    44|          SERVICE_ID="${{ secrets.RENDER_SERVICE_ID }}"
    45|          CLEAR_CACHE="${{ github.event.inputs.clear_cache == 'true' && 'clear' || 'do_not_clear' }}"
    46|
    47|          echo "Triggering Render deploy for service: ${SERVICE_ID}"
    48|          echo "GitHub SHA: ${{ github.sha }}"
    49|
    50|          RESPONSE=$(curl -sS --connect-timeout 10 --max-time 30 \
    51|            -X POST "https://api.render.com/v1/services/${SERVICE_ID}/deploys" \
    52|            -H "Authorization: Bearer ${{ secrets.RENDER_API_KEY }}" \
    53|            -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
    54|            -H "Accept: application/json" \
    55|            -d "{\"clearCache\": \"${CLEAR_CACHE}\"}")
    56|
    57|          echo "Render API response:"
    58|          echo "$RESPONSE" | python3 -m json.tool 2>/dev/null || echo "$RESPONSE"
    59|
    60|          DEPLOY_ID=$(echo "$RESPONSE" | python3 -c "
    61|          import sys,json
    62|          d = json.load(sys.stdin)
    63|          deploy = d.get('deploy', d)
    64|          print(deploy.get('id', ''))
    65|          " 2>/dev/null || echo "")
    66|
    67|          DEPLOY_STATUS=$(echo "$RESPONSE" | python3 -c "
    68|          import sys,json
    69|          d = json.load(sys.stdin)
    70|          deploy = d.get('deploy', d)
    71|          print(deploy.get('status', ''))
    72|          " 2>/dev/null || echo "")
    73|
    74|          echo "deploy_id=${DEPLOY_ID}" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
    75|          echo "deploy_status=${DEPLOY_STATUS}" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
    76|
    77|          if [ -n "${DEPLOY_ID}" ]; then
    78|            echo "Deploy triggered: ${DEPLOY_ID} (${DEPLOY_STATUS})"
    79|          else
    80|            echo "::error::Could not parse deploy ID from Render response"
    81|            exit 1
    82|          fi
    83|
    84|      - name: Wait for Deploy
    85|        if: steps.deploy.outputs.deploy_id != ''
    86|        id: wait
    87|        run: |
    88|          DEPLOY_ID="${{ steps.deploy.outputs.deploy_id }}"
    89|          SERVICE_ID="${{ secrets.RENDER_SERVICE_ID }}"
    90|          MAX_WAIT=600
    91|          WAITED=0
    92|          INTERVAL=10
    93|
    94|          echo "Waiting for deploy ${DEPLOY_ID} to complete..."
    95|          while [ $WAITED -lt $MAX_WAIT ]; do
    96|            RESPONSE=$(curl -sS --connect-timeout 10 --max-time 15 \
    97|              "https://api.render.com/v1/services/${SERVICE_ID}/deploys/${DEPLOY_ID}" \
    98|              -H "Authorization: Bearer ${{ secrets.RENDER_API_KEY }}" \
    99|              -H "Accept: application/json")
   100|
   101|            STATUS=$(echo "$RESPONSE" | python3 -c "
   102|            import sys,json
   103|            d = json.load(sys.stdin)
   104|            print(d.get('status', ''))
   105|            " 2>/dev/null || echo "unknown")
   106|
   107|            COMMIT_SHA=$(echo "$RESPONSE" | python3 -c "
   108|            import sys,json
   109|            d = json.load(sys.stdin)
   110|            commit = d.get('commit', {}) or {}
   111|            sha = commit.get('id', '') if isinstance(commit, dict) else ''
   112|            print(sha[:8] if sha else '?')
   113|            " 2>/dev/null || echo "?")
   114|
   115|            echo "[${WAITED}s] Status: ${STATUS} | Commit: ${COMMIT_SHA}"
   116|
   117|            case "${STATUS}" in
   118|              live)
   119|                echo "Deploy is LIVE at commit ${COMMIT_SHA}!"
   120|                echo "deploy_commit=${COMMIT_SHA}" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
   121|                echo "deploy_final_status=live" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
   122|                exit 0
   123|                ;;
   124|              build_failed|update_failed|canceled|deactivated)
   125|                echo "::error::Deploy failed: ${STATUS}"
   126|                echo "deploy_final_status=${STATUS}" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
   127|                exit 1
   128|                ;;
   129|            esac
   130|
   131|            sleep $INTERVAL
   132|            WAITED=$((WAITED + INTERVAL))
   133|          done
   134|
   135|          echo "::error::Timeout waiting for deploy (${MAX_WAIT}s)"
   136|          echo "deploy_final_status=timeout" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
   137|          exit 1
   138|
   139|      - name: Verify Production Health
   140|        if: always()
   141|        run: |
   142|          echo "Checking production health..."
   143|          GITHUB_SHA_SHORT=$(echo "${{ github.sha }}" | cut -c1-8)
   144|
   145|          for i in 1 2 3 4 5; do
   146|            HEALTH=$(curl -sS --connect-timeout 10 --max-time 15 \
   147|              https://api.ivxholding.com/health 2>/dev/null || echo '{"status":"unreachable"}')
   148|
   149|            STATUS=$(echo "$HEALTH" | python3 -c "
   150|            import sys,json
   151|            print(json.load(sys.stdin).get('status','?'))
   152|            " 2>/dev/null || echo "?")
   153|
   154|            COMMIT=$(echo "$HEALTH" | python3 -c "
   155|            import sys,json
   156|            print(json.load(sys.stdin).get('commitShort','?'))
   157|            " 2>/dev/null || echo "?")
   158|
   159|            echo "Health check $i: status=${STATUS} commit=${COMMIT}"
   160|
   161|            if [ "$STATUS" = "healthy" ]; then
   162|              if [ "$COMMIT" = "${GITHUB_SHA_SHORT}" ]; then
   163|                echo "COMMIT_MATCH: YES — production commit ${COMMIT} matches GitHub ${GITHUB_SHA_SHORT}"
   164|              else
   165|                echo "COMMIT_MATCH: NO — production=${COMMIT} github=${GITHUB_SHA_SHORT}"
   166|              fi
   167|              break
   168|            fi
   169|            sleep 10
   170|          done
   171|
   172|      - name: Summary
   173|        if: always()
   174|        run: |
   175|          {
   176|            echo "## Render Auto-Deploy Summary"
   177|            echo ""
   178|            echo "| Field | Value |"
   179|            echo "|-------|-------|"
   180|            echo "| GitHub SHA | \`${{ github.sha }}\` |"
   181|            echo "| Deploy ID | \`${{ steps.deploy.outputs.deploy_id || 'N/A' }}\` |"
   182|            echo "| Deploy Status | \`${{ steps.wait.outputs.deploy_final_status || steps.deploy.outputs.deploy_status || 'N/A' }}\` |"
   183|            echo "| Deployed Commit | \`${{ steps.wait.outputs.deploy_commit || 'N/A' }}\` |"
   184|            echo "| Trigger | \`${{ github.event_name }}\` |"
   185|          } >> $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY

Done — the scroll-up issue is fixed and live on `ivxholding.com`.

## What was wrong

The landing page CSS had `body { overscroll-behavior-y: none; }`. That rule disables the browser’s normal overscroll/rubber-band behavior, which on mobile Chrome/Safari makes the page feel “stuck” when the user tries to scroll up or pull-to-refresh at the top of the page.

## What I changed

- Removed `overscroll-behavior-y: none` from `body` in `expo/ivxholding-landing/index.html`.
- Replaced it with a comment explaining why the rule was removed so it isn’t reintroduced.

## Deployed live

- Commit pushed to `main`: `5b93f500a51894522f2c6ed9103168da144a6671`
- GitHub Actions workflow run `#36` built and uploaded the new `index.html` to S3: https://github.com/ibb142/rork-global-real-estate-invest/actions/runs/28752374325
- S3 object was updated at `Sun, 05 Jul 2026 19:33:27 GMT`.

## Live proof

Verified right now against both CloudFront and the S3 origin:

| Source | URL | Active `overscroll-behavior-y: none` on `body` | Fix present |
|--------|-----|-----------------------------------------------|-------------|
| CloudFront | `https://ivxholding.com/` | `false` | `true` |
| S3 origin | `https://s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/ivxholding.com/index.html` | `false` | `true` |

The live HTML now contains:
```css
/* overscroll-behavior-y: none; REMOVED — it blocked pull-to-refresh and made scroll-up feel stuck on mobile */
```

## One follow-up item

The workflow logs show CloudFront invalidation is being skipped because the AWS IAM user (`Rork1`) does not have `cloudfront:CreateInvalidation` permission. The fix still propagated this time because S3 was updated and the edge cache refreshed, but for future instant deploys you should add that permission to the IAM policy attached to the GitHub Actions AWS key.
